rulings.question: What is the skill-graph identity boundary?
rulings.ruling: Keep `sk-doc` as one hub skill-graph node: one hub-level `graph-metadata.json`, one hub-level `description.json`, no child packet graph metadata or child advisor descriptions.
rulings.rationale: The advisor rebuild path persists rows from authored skill metadata, while the reference hub keeps one root `skill_id` and represents nested packets as root `key_files`/`source_docs`; `sk-doc` already has the single root `skill_id` that must remain the only advisor identity.

rulings.question: How must `derived` span the workflow-only hub?
rulings.ruling: Rewrite root `derived.trigger_phrases`, `key_topics`, `intent_signals`, `source_docs`, `key_files`, `entities`, and `causal_summary` to cover every retained workflow packet plus `shared/`, not the old monolith mode list.
rulings.rationale: Current `sk-doc` derived metadata is centered on readme/install-guide/flowchart/catalog and old root paths; the reference hub records shared references and nested packet files from the root graph metadata.

rulings.question: Should advisor metadata point at facade symlinks or canonical nested paths?
rulings.ruling: Point graph metadata at canonical nested packet and `shared/` paths; reserve root facade symlinks for external compatibility only.
rulings.rationale: The reference hub cites `mode-registry.json`, `hub-router.json`, `shared/...`, and nested packet paths directly, while current `sk-doc` still cites monolith-era root references, assets, and scripts.

rulings.question: What should `description.json` contain?
rulings.ruling: Create a hub-level `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/description.json` with `name`, hub description, version, importance tier, workflow keywords, trigger examples, and `lastUpdated`; do not model individual workflows as separate descriptions.
rulings.rationale: The reference hub exposes advisor-facing keywords and trigger examples in a root description while keeping the root graph as the single skill identity.

rulings.question: Which explicit advisor booster terms are missing for the new packet set?
rulings.ruling: Add `sk-doc` explicit-lane boosts for `benchmark` if kept, `command`/`create-command`, `feature-catalog` and `feature catalog`, `changelog` if kept, and `skill`/`create-skill`/`create skill`/`create parent skill`; target `sk-doc` only.
rulings.rationale: Current explicit boosts cover `docs`, `documentation`, `playbook`, `readme`, and `taxonomy`, plus agent/testing-playbook phrases; `feature_catalog` only appears in the generic write-doc regex, and `opencode skill`/`opencode command` currently boost `sk-code` instead of `sk-doc`.

rulings.question: How should existing create-command aliases be handled?
rulings.ruling: Do not introduce packet-shaped advisor skill IDs; normalize existing `/create:*` phrase coverage so final recommendations resolve to the single `sk-doc` skill, even when command aliases remain as compatibility evidence.
rulings.rationale: Current phrase boosts include non-hub targets such as `create:agent`, `create:testing-playbook`, and `command-create-testing-playbook`; the graph identity must remain the root `sk-doc` node.

risks: If `derived.key_files` and `source_docs` keep monolith paths, advisor evidence can point at compatibility facades instead of canonical workflow packets: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/graph-metadata.json:98-107,159-165; .opencode/skills/sk-code/graph-metadata.json:182-220

Without high-specificity `sk-doc` boosts for skill/command authoring, prompts containing `opencode skill` or `opencode command` can remain anchored to `sk-code`: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/scorer/lanes/explicit.ts:221-223

Existing alias-shaped boosts make the one-skill invariant less legible unless tests assert final recommendation `skillId` is `sk-doc`: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/scorer/lanes/explicit.ts:101-103,204-209; .opencode/skills/sk-doc/graph-metadata.json:3
open: Decide keep-or-fold for `create-benchmark` and `create-changelog` before finalizing trigger phrases; current `sk-doc` graph metadata has no benchmark/changelog coverage: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/graph-metadata.json:65-78

Audit all `/create:*` command phrases after the architecture decision; current explicit phrase coverage only names agent and testing-playbook create paths: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/scorer/lanes/explicit.ts:101-103,204-209
